summary: Norway has established four new quantum technology research centers, with a combined funding of 244 million Norwegian kroner from the Research Council of Norway. The centers, led by Simula Research Laboratory, University of Oslo, and SINTEF, will focus on quantum computing, communication, sensors, and quantum software. This move is part of a national strategy, with a planned total investment of 1.1 billion Norwegian kroner over five years, including 100 new university study places. The new centers will collaborate internationally with institutions such as the Niels Bohr Institute and industry leaders like Telenor and Toshiba. Minister Sigrun Aasland described this as Norway joining "the international quantum race," while Minister Tore O. Sandvik highlighted its significance for national security.
